label: Model-predictive control

node.label: Model-predictive control
node.definition: Model-predictive control repeatedly optimizes a finite-horizon action sequence under a dynamics model, executes the first action, and replans from new observations.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Model-predictive control is modeled as depending on Dynamical systems & control theory, Optimization theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Robotics & embodied AI.
examples: atlas downstream link to Robotics & embodied AI
combined with Dynamical systems & control theory
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Dynamical systems & control theory, Optimization theory.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Model-predictive control has a `uses` dependency on Dynamical systems & control theory because Dynamical systems & control theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Model-predictive control has a `uses` dependency on Optimization theory because Optimization theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Model-predictive control has a `uses` dependency on Latent world models & predictive dynamics because Latent world models & predictive dynamics is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
